beats.narration_text: Vanakkam — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: duration-planner, the skill that answers how long a video should be — by content, not by clock. Duration is an output here, not a target.

props.runningText: sizing each beat to content, not clock...

beats.narration_text: The skill reads a beat sheet and a timings file. It has a consolidation floor table per content type, a pace-check script, and one evidence reference. The two output levers are script-sizing — how many beats an idea gets — and holds: extending on-screen time past the narration for consolidation.

props.title: Two levers. One floor table.
files: SKILL.md — consolidation floor table + two levers

beats.narration_text: Five steps: count interacting elements per beat — if more than one new thing must be held simultaneously, split it; read each beat's content-type and look up its consolidation floor; check real durations against the floor; recommend holds for below-floor beats; flag over-ceiling beats for splitting. Report total runtime as an output.

props.title: Count → floor → check → hold → report.
props.outputLabel: holds recommended + splits flagged + total runtime

phases.label: COUNT
phases.note: >1 interacting element per beat → split it

phases.label: CHECK
phases.note: pace_check.py: narration vs floor; above ceiling → split signal

phases.label: HOLD
phases.note: below-floor beats get a hold to reach floor — never stretch narration

phases.label: REPORT
phases.note: total runtime as output — if clock target conflicts, name the cost

props.footerNote: Audio-first: durations are real Kokoro/ElevenLabs MP3 lengths — never assigned manually.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: pace-check two beats from the sim-scout video — the self-demo ask and the self-demo result.

Beat B03 is a brief ask — title-type content, consolidation floor three to five seconds. Narration is six-oh-four seconds: above floor, pass. Recommend one-second inter-beat hold at the idea boundary. Beat B04 is mechanism content — floor six to ten seconds, ceiling around twenty. Narration is twenty-point-one seconds: above floor, at the ceiling. It reads as one complex idea — a single card with two predictions. No split. Total video: one forty-nine point three seconds. The content derived this; no clock was consulted.

props.title: PACE-CHECK — claude-liam-sim-scout
props.code:
B03 "self-demo ask"  actual: 6.04s
  content_type: title/definitional
  floor: 3–5s  →  ABOVE FLOOR ✓
  action: +1s inter-beat hold (idea boundary)

B04 "self-demo result"  actual: 20.12s
  content_type: mechanism
  floor: 6–10s  ceiling: ~20s  →  ABOVE FLOOR ✓
  at-ceiling check: one complex idea (card +
  two predictions) — genuinely single beat
  action: none — no split needed

total runtime: 149.3s
  → content derives this; no clock consulted

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: duration is an output. A uniform thirty-second or one-minute target has no learning basis — it is a production convenience. Forcing content into it either compresses, which destroys integration, or pads, which adds extraneous load. A three-minute mechanism video and a thirty-second definitional clip are both correct. The clock did not decide either.

props.heading: The clock is not the spec.
props.body: A uniform target compresses multi-element content (destroying integration) or pads simple content (adding extraneous load). Both degrade learning. The right derivation: count the beats the idea needs, add holds where the consolidation floor isn't met, report the sum. A complex mechanism lands at 3–4 min; a definition lands at 30 s. Both are correct.
props.quote: Duration is an output, never a target.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: the consolidation floor. Working memory needs a minimum window to register and begin integrating a new element. The floor depends on content type: a title needs three to five seconds; a mechanism step needs six to ten; an equation step needs seven to twelve. Cutting below the floor does not make learning faster. It makes the element fail to register.

props.heading: Floor, not aesthetic.
props.body: Consolidation floors by content_type: title 3–5s · realworld 4–5s · structure/geometric 6–8s · data 6–8s · mechanism 6–10s · equation 7–12s. These are working-memory constraints, not pacing preferences. A narration that runs 4s on a mechanism beat needs a hold to reach 6s — not a faster voice or a shortened next beat.
props.quote: Cutting a beat below the time working memory needs to register and begin integrating the new element does not make learning faster — it makes it fail.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: padding is a coherence violation. Once the idea is presented and narration is done, adding decorative motion, filler graphics, or silence to hit a number adds extraneous load. Extraneous load is the one kind that always degrades learning — it competes with germane processing without adding anything. The ceiling on a beat is: the idea is complete. Then cut.

props.heading: Cut when the idea is done.
props.body: Padding to reach a clock target is a coherence violation: it adds extraneous load (the worst kind — it competes with germane processing without contributing). The 6-minute rule is a watch-time finding, not a learning result, and it doesn't replicate in enrolled courses. Correct length = structural completeness. No more, no less.
props.quote: Optimize for the structural completeness of each beat, not completion rate.

beats.narration_text: Duration-planner: content derives the length, the consolidation floor sets the minimum, and the ceiling is the idea being complete. The clock is never the spec.

Your turn. Run duration-planner on a beat sheet. Before you accept any duration: name the content-type of each beat. Check whether any beat's narration is below the consolidation floor for that type. If any is — add a hold. If the total runtime is longer than expected — that is the content talking, not the planner failing.

props.runningText: name the content_type of each beat first
output: name content_type of every beat before checking
flag any beat below its consolidation floor
add holds — never stretch narration or speed voice
if runtime is long — that is the content, not a mistake

beats.narration_text: Claude, Timed. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
